data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism's sufficient condition (P > e AND R > 1-e) does not guarantee net benefit because it conflates the candidate error rate e with the critic's base rate on fragile pools. The claim requires beating SC on the subset of pools with majority margin ≤2. For k=7 at e≈0.25, the fraction of fragile pools that are incorrect (P_inc) is roughly 0.29. The condition R > 1-e = 0.75 and P > e = 0.25 is evaluated over ALL candidates, not specifically conditioned on the fragile subset. A critic could easily achieve P > 0.25 globally while having a catastrophic false positive rate specifically on correct-fragile majorities, breaking the P_cor/N_cor balance. The algebraic leap from global precision/recall bounds to subset-net-benefit ignores base rate shifts within the fragile subset, failing to design a decisive prediction.
